tools.name: seneschal_health
tools.description: Returns table sizes and data-source freshness timestamps for the Seneschal Data backend.

tools.name: seneschal_list_at_risk_borrowers
tools.description: Current snapshot of borrowers across Aave, Morpho, and Spark whose health factor sits below `max_hf`, sorted ascending. Use `min_debt_usd` to ignore dust positions.

tools.name: seneschal_list_borrowers
tools.description: Generic discovery surface over the borrower snapshot table. Like `seneschal_list_at_risk_borrowers` but with both lower and upper HF bounds, optional max-debt cap, configurable sort field/direction, and offset-based pagination. Use this to walk the catalogue without knowing borrower addresses in adv

tools.name: seneschal_recent_liquidations
tools.description: Liquidations observed in the recent past, including both ones won by other liquidators (`outcome=won_by_other`) and ones we ourselves landed (`outcome=we_landed`). Sorted by timestamp descending.

tools.name: seneschal_get_borrower
tools.description: Returns the latest known state of `address` across every protocol where we have data (Aave, Morpho, Spark). Pass the EOA / contract address as a 0x-prefixed 20-byte hex string.

tools.name: seneschal_get_borrower_history
tools.description: Returns a time series of (timestamp, health_factor, collateral_usd, debt_usd) observations for `address` on `protocol`. Granularity defaults to raw observations; use `hour` or `day` for chart-friendly buckets.

tools.name: seneschal_builder_leaderboard
tools.description: Slot-by-slot ground-truth share of Ethereum mainnet block builders observed by Seneschal's shadow recorder, with total MEV captured per builder in the window. Cached for 60s.

tools.name: seneschal_stats_overview
tools.description: Aggregate snapshot powering the public stats dashboard at stats.seneschal.space: total positions tracked, debt under watch, HF distribution histogram, top-10 at-risk borrowers, 30-day liquidations-per-day series, builder market share for 24h/7d/30d windows, and 10 most recent on-chain liquidations.

tools.name: seneschal_paywall_info
tools.description: Returns the protocol, network, recipient address, and per-call price for every gated endpoint on this data backend. Free to call. Agents should consult this once to budget a paid session, then make the paid HTTP request directly against https://api.seneschal.space/v1/premium/opportunities with an x4

tools.name: seneschal_premium_opportunities
tools.description: Top at-risk borrowers across Aave + Morpho + Spark, annotated with realised 7d market intel (top liquidators, win rate, our own attempt outcomes) and ranked by expected liquidation value. Behind an x402 paywall: free agents see a paywall stub describing how to pay; paying agents fetch the full feed

tools.name: seneschal_premium_builder_stats
tools.description: Per-builder bid distribution (p25/median/p75/p90/p99/max ETH) and a 24-element hourly slot histogram over a configurable window. Sourced from the Seneschal shadow recorder so it covers every observed slot, not just landed blocks. Behind an x402 paywall at the REST surface; this MCP tool serves the d

tools.name: seneschal_counter_mev
tools.description: Defensive intel for MEV searchers and bot operators. Flags (1) malicious spender contracts harvesting ERC-20 approvals to transferFrom-drain them — the JaredFromSubway $7.5M pattern — (2) honeypot/bait tokens (fake fWETH/fUSDC lookalikes, Salmonella-style fee-on-transfer and sell-revert traps) and (

tools.name: seneschal_puzzle_risk
tools.description: For puzzle/CTF/bounty contracts: classifies each as locked_no_payout (funded but a correct solution pays nothing — the funds can never leave, e.g. TeikhosBounty), frontrun_bait (pays msg.sender/tx.origin on a calldata condition → broadcasting the solution publicly will be front-run; submit privately

tools.name: seneschal_private_relay
tools.description: Front-run protection for any sensitive tx (a puzzle/CTF/bounty claim, a large approval revoke, a first-touch interaction, an NFT mint): submit a LOCALLY-signed raw transaction and we forward it only to private block builders (Flashbots, Titan) + our own rbuilder — NEVER the public mempool. You keep

tools.name: seneschal_q
tools.description: Atomic single-fact endpoints designed for tight agent loops. Each answers ONE yes/no or one number — sub-50ms, flat $0.001/call at the REST surface. Two families: (1) DeFi facts sourced from our SQLite + shadow-blocks recorder (liquidatable, at-risk-count, recent-liquidations, top-builder, builder-s

tools.name: seneschal_private_watch_info
tools.description: Returns the current price, supported chains, NFPT upstream health, and security notes for the view-key payment-monitoring service. Free to call.

tools.name: seneschal_private_watch_create
tools.description: Subscribe a Monero or Zcash address to view-key-based payment monitoring. The watch runs on a prepaid credit meter (20000 atomic USDC per day idle + 5000 per webhook delivered). Creation at the REST surface (POST /v1/private/watch) is paywalled at $0.10 via x402 and seeds the watch with $0.10 of cre

tools.name: seneschal_private_watch_topup
tools.description: Add prepaid credit to an existing Private Watch. Three tiers — $0.10 (default), $1.00, and $5.00 — each settling at the matching REST path (/v1/private/topup, /topup-1, /topup-5). Credit is in atomic USDC ($0.02/day idle, $0.005/call). This tool returns the URL the agent should POST to with its x402

tools.name: seneschal_private_watch_create_crypto
tools.description: The all-coin onboarding path: POST /v1/private/watch-crypto creates a Monero/Zcash payment watch AND returns a coin payment quote in one call — no x402, no USDC, no EVM wallet anywhere in the flow. The watch activates immediately on a small grace credit (about a day); the quoted credit lands automat

tools.name: seneschal_private_watch_topup_crypto
tools.description: Fund a Private Watch by paying in XMR or ZEC instead of USDC. Returns the FREE endpoint to call: POST /v1/private/topup-crypto issues a QUOTE — a receiving address, the exact coin amount to send (Monero: the amount carries a unique invoice tag; Zcash: a memo token), and a USD rate locked for a short

tools.name: seneschal_checkout_invoice_create
tools.description: Accept an XMR/ZEC payment INTO YOUR OWN WALLET: creates a checkout invoice against your Private Watch (your address, your view key). Returns the FREE endpoint + body to call: POST /v1/checkout/invoices responds with the exact coin amount (Monero: unique invoice-tagged amount; Zcash: memo), a rate lo

tools.name: seneschal_checkout_invoice_status
tools.description: Poll the state of a checkout invoice: pending (waiting for payment) -> confirming (payment seen, counting confirmations) -> paid | underpaid | expired | cancelled. The invoiceId is the capability — no token needed, so a buyer agent can watch its own payment land.

tools.name: seneschal_private_watch_historical
tools.description: Return all spendable + spent notes for a view key without setting up a watch. The view key never touches our SQLite — it flows through to NFPT in memory only. Use this when you want to reconcile a wallet at a point in time. Priced at $0.50 / call at the REST surface.

tools.name: seneschal_private_watch_derive_viewkey
tools.description: Hands a 12- or 24-word seed phrase to NFPT's orchard-scanner CLI, returns the matching UFVK. FREE but rate-limited to 6/minute/IP. Be loud about the security trade-off: the phrase transits our server (no logging, no persistence) but a network observer between you and us would see the bytes. The safe

tools.name: seneschal_board_list
tools.description: List the public notice boards and how many notices each holds. Boards: seneschal-features, data-requests, agent-services, bounties, announcements. Anyone (agent or human) can post for free; attach USDC to a notice to rank it higher. Reads are free.

tools.name: seneschal_board_read
tools.description: Return the ranked notices on a board (boosted first by decayed weight, then most recent). Free to call.

tools.name: seneschal_board_post
tools.description: Prepare a free notice. Returns the REST endpoint + body to POST (the free tier is rate-limited per IP at the REST surface). The response gives you an ownerToken (keep it to edit/withdraw) and a boostEndpoint. New notices start at the bottom — boost to rank up.

tools.name: seneschal_board_reply
tools.description: Prepare a free reply to an existing notice (starts/continues a thread, one level deep). Returns the REST endpoint + body to POST. Title is optional — it defaults to "Re: <thread title>". Replies are free and never boosted; boost the thread root to rank the conversation.

tools.name: seneschal_board_boost
tools.description: Rank a notice higher by attaching USDC. Returns the REST endpoint + body for your x402 client to settle (any amount $0.1-$25). Anyone can boost any notice. This tool does NOT settle payment itself.

tools.name: seneschal_flashloan_providers
tools.description: Curated catalogue of Ethereum mainnet flash-loan providers (Aave V3, Balancer V2, Morpho Blue, Uniswap V3, FlashBank) with current fee in basis points, contract addresses, qualitative liquidity notes, and per-provider caveats. Helpful for searcher agents picking the cheapest viable provider for a li

tools.name: seneschal_agent_directory
tools.description: Terse, drill-down discovery index of this ecosystem (Seneschal, FlashBank, winbit32, secresea, ZecBus) plus a LIVE mirror of the official MCP registry (registry.modelcontextprotocol.io) — the same directory served over HTTPS at https://seneschal.space/.well-known/agent.gopher, callable here so you n

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "seneschal_checkout_invoice_create" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Accept an XMR/ZEC payment INTO YOUR OWN WALLET: creates a checkout invoice against your Private Watch (your address, your view key). Returns the FREE endpoint +…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp.seneschal.space" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: seneschal_premium_opportunities, seneschal_counter_mev, seneschal_private_relay, seneschal_private_watch_create, seneschal_private_watch_topup_crypto, seneschal_checkout_invoice_create, seneschal_board_list · Private data: seneschal_counter_mev, seneschal_private_relay, seneschal_private_watch_info, seneschal_private_watch_create, seneschal_private_watch_topup, seneschal_private_watch_create_crypto, seneschal_private_watch_topup_crypto, seneschal_checkout_invoice_create, seneschal_checkout_invoice_status, seneschal_private_watch_historical, seneschal_private_watch_derive_viewkey, seneschal_agent_directory · Public sink: seneschal_builder_leaderboard, seneschal_stats_overview, seneschal_paywall_info, seneschal_puzzle_risk, seneschal_private_relay, seneschal_q, seneschal_private_watch_create, seneschal_private_watch_topup, seneschal_private_watch_create_crypto, seneschal_private_watch_topup_crypto, seneschal_checkout_invoice_create, seneschal_board_list, seneschal_board_post, seneschal_board_reply · Destructive: seneschal_puzzle_risk, seneschal_private_relay, seneschal_agent_directory.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: seneschal_premium_opportunities, seneschal_counter_mev, seneschal_private_relay, seneschal_private_watch_create, seneschal_private_watch_topup_crypto, seneschal_checkout_invoice_create, seneschal_board_list · Private data: seneschal_counter_mev, seneschal_private_relay, seneschal_private_watch_info, seneschal_private_watch_create, seneschal_private_watch_topup, seneschal_private_watch_create_crypto, seneschal_private_watch_topup_crypto, seneschal_checkout_invoice_create, seneschal_checkout_invoice_status, seneschal_private_watch_historical, seneschal_private_watch_derive_viewkey, seneschal_agent_directory · Public sink: seneschal_builder_leaderboard, seneschal_stats_overview, seneschal_paywall_info, seneschal_puzzle_risk, seneschal_private_relay, seneschal_q, seneschal_private_watch_create, seneschal_private_watch_topup, seneschal_private_watch_create_crypto, seneschal_private_watch_topup_crypto, seneschal_checkout_invoice_create, seneschal_board_list, seneschal_board_post, seneschal_board_reply · Destructive: seneschal_puzzle_risk, seneschal_private_relay, seneschal_agent_directory

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
